_comment: Which external bodies this mission introduces, and what they orbit. That is
all: `lunco-celestial-ephemeris` reads it to place NAIF -1024 under the Earth
and to know which cached CSV belongs to it.
HOW the vectors are obtained is NOT here. The JPL Horizons query is a declared
lunco-assets on an explicit user request (Settings ▸ Downloadable data).
Nothing fetches at startup. This file used to carry the full query too —
command / ref_plane / start_time / stop_time / step_size — which was a second
copy of the recipe and the reason the app opened a connection on launch.
Until that dataset is downloaded the mission simply has no trajectory:
`position()` answers None and the spacecraft is not drawn. That is the
intended offline behaviour, not an error.
This file used to also carry Artemis II's trajectory colours, sampling rates
and spacecraft marker sizes, and MissionPlugin spawned all of it into any
scene that declared a celestial body. That visualisation config is now USD
(assets/missions/artemis_2_mission.usda) and is opted into per scene.
If committed kernels ever replace the download, the right format is CCSDS OEM
(502.0-B KVN) or SPICE SPK — at which point the dataset entry is replaced by
the asset itself rather than a recipe for downloading it.
